description: Phone classifier rules. Pure EXIF — no lens-registry dependence. Brand-specific encoding (Apple FocusDistanceRange in MakerNotes, XMP RegionType for faces, front-camera signal in LensModel) is absorbed by the per-brand profile in assets/brand_profiles/; rules query brand-agnostic concepts. v3 (2026-05-13) adds phone_close_focus — fires when the lens's focus position is near minimum distance, catching iPhone macro shots that don't carry the keyword 'macro' anywhere. Bumping forces a re-seed of the user override on next launch. v4 (2026-05-28) adds phone_default_street — phones are implicit-auto by device class; casual shots (no face, no macro, no bracket) default to street rather than the GENERAL+needs_review path that was producing 81%+ flag-noise on the Nepal 2025 corpus.

rules.description: Front camera shot = selfie. iPhone writes 'iPhone XX front camera Y.YYmm f/Z.Z' in LensModel; the polymorphic `contains` operator picks up the substring case-insensitively. Validated 100% on 8 iPhone HEIC samples (2026-04-29).

then.reason: Front camera lens detected in EXIF LensModel

rules.description: Phone lens focused at very close range. Apple writes FocusDistanceRange in MakerNotes (e.g. '0.12 - 0.16 m' for a true macro); the apple profile parses the upper bound (closest discriminator on Apple — hyperfocal landscapes have a close lower bound but a far upper bound) and normalizes it over max_meters=1.5. Threshold 0.2 corresponds to upper bound < 0.3m, which separates genuine macro from close portraits / pets / hyperfocal landscapes. Verified on IMG_3475 macro 2026-05-13.
Focus bracket mode on phone = macro stacking work. Same intent signal as camera T1.1.
Exposure bracket mode on phone = HDR landscape. Same intent signal as camera T1.2.
Shutter ≥ 1s. iPhone Night mode commonly produces shutters of 1-3s when triggered.
Face detected via XMP RegionType. The Apple brand profile maps RegionType containing 'Face' to subject_detection=human. Fires when at least one face was registered in the photo.

Casual phone shot — none of the above rules fired (no front camera, no close focus, no bracket, no long exposure, no face). Phones are implicit-auto by device class — the user delegated every decision to the device, so they were not deliberating about a specialized genre. Default to street (casual urban / travel snap) with low confidence + needs_review so the user can override the cases where it's actually a landscape, food shot, etc. Replaces the prior fallback path that dumped ~80% of casual iPhone shots into GENERAL with needs_review (Nepal 2025-10-27 to 2025-11-04 corpus: 81%+ of non-selfie phone shots hit this empty-default path). The when:{} block matches everything, so this MUST be the last rule in the file.
